review_notes: The functions accurately map SOC2 CC6.3 control requirements to Kubernetes RBAC configurations. The naming is mostly clear and follows conventions, but there is room for slight improvements in clarity and specificity.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_rbac_role_separation_of_duties' to 'k8s_rbac_role_separation_enforced' for better clarity on the action being checked.
title: CC6.3 The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives
step1_reasoning: The SOC2 control CC6.3 focuses on managing access based on roles and responsibilities, emphasizing least privilege and segregation of duties. In Kubernetes, this directly maps to RBAC configurations. Ensuring no cluster-wide admin bindings, enforcing least privilege, and maintaining role separation are crucial to achieving these objectives. Additionally, restricting service account privileges ensures that automated processes adhere to the same principles.
